documents.title: Highlights
documents.summary: This document provides an overview of the major version releases and feature highlights for the Ratatui library starting from version 0.21.0.

documents.title: Ratatui
documents.description: Ratatui: Cook up delicious terminal user interfaces in Rust - the fast and lightweight TUI library trusted by developers
documents.summary: Ratatui is a Rust library designed for building high-performance terminal user interfaces with features like immediate-mode rendering, rich widgets, and responsive layouts.

documents.title: App Showcase
documents.summary: This document provides a collection of terminal-based applications and command-line utilities designed for system monitoring, development, and data management. It showcases various interactive tools that enhance productivity within the terminal environment using specialized user interfaces.

documents.title: Built-in Widgets Showcase
documents.summary: This document provides an overview of the built-in widgets available in the Ratatui library, offering links to official documentation and example code for each terminal UI component.

documents.title: Showcase
documents.summary: A collection of links showcasing applications and widgets built with the Ratatui terminal user interface library.

documents.title: Feature Flags
documents.summary: This document explains how to configure the Ratatui library by selecting specific terminal backends and enabling optional features like the calendar widget and Serde support.

documents.title: Installation
documents.summary: This document provides instructions for installing the ratatui Rust crate and configuring its optional backend features using Cargo.

documents.title: Component Template
documents.summary: This document explains how to use and understand an opinionated Ratatui project template for creating terminal user interfaces with Rust and Tokio. It covers project initialization, dependency integration, and the architectural design of component-based async applications.

documents.title: Ratatui Templates
documents.summary: This document explains how to initialize a new Ratatui application using pre-defined templates and the cargo-generate tool.

documents.title: Hello Ratatui
documents.summary: A step-by-step tutorial for creating a basic 'Hello World' terminal user interface application using Rust and the Ratatui library.

documents.title: Demo
documents.summary: This document provides the source code and setup instructions for the original Ratatui demo application across multiple terminal backends. It demonstrates how to structure a Rust terminal UI application using stateful widgets, data signals, and event handling.

documents.title: Demo 2
documents.summary: This document provides instructions for cloning the Ratatui repository and running the demo2 example application to showcase the library's terminal user interface capabilities.

documents.title: Docs.rs
documents.summary: This document provides example Rust code for the Ratatui library, demonstrating how to implement terminal user interfaces with layouts, text styling, and event handling.

documents.title: Hello World
documents.summary: This document provides a minimal example of a terminal user interface application using Ratatui, demonstrating basic setup, rendering, and event loop management.

documents.title: Inline Viewport
documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to implement an inline terminal user interface using Ratatui's inline viewport feature to render progress bars and handle background tasks without taking over the full screen.

documents.title: Minimal Hello World
documents.summary: This document provides a minimal starter example for building terminal user interfaces with the Ratatui library in Rust, demonstrating basic terminal initialization and frame rendering.

documents.title: Panic Hooks
documents.summary: This document explains how to handle panics in Ratatui applications to ensure the terminal state is correctly restored when a crash occurs. It demonstrates the automatic panic hook setup provided by the library and how it differs from manual or disabled hook states.

documents.title: Popup
documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to use the Clear widget in Ratatui to create popup overlays by clearing a specific area of the terminal before rendering a new widget on top.

documents.title: Ratatui Logo
documents.summary: This document provides a code example for rendering the Ratatui logo in a terminal using half-block graphics and built-in library widgets.

documents.title: User Input
documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to implement and handle user text input, cursor movement, and state-based input modes in a Ratatui terminal application.

documents.title: App Examples
documents.summary: This document provides a collection of example applications and demo projects showcasing the capabilities of the Ratatui terminal user interface library.

documents.title: Constraint Explorer
documents.summary: An interactive utility and code example for visualizing and exploring how different layout constraints interact within the Ratatui terminal UI library.

documents.title: Constraints
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive code example demonstrating how to use different layout constraints in Ratatui to organize UI elements, covering Min, Max, Length, Percentage, Ratio, and Fill.

documents.title: Flex
documents.summary: This document demonstrates the Flex layout system in the Ratatui library, illustrating how different constraints and alignment strategies affect the distribution of space in a terminal UI.

documents.title: Layout
documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to use the Ratatui layout system to partition terminal screen space using various geometric constraints. It provides a functional implementation for organizing UI components through nested vertical and horizontal layouts.

documents.title: Layout Examples
documents.summary: This document provides a directory of examples and tools for managing terminal user interface layouts and constraints using the Ratatui library.

documents.title: Colors (RGB)
documents.summary: This document explains how to use 24-bit RGB colors in Ratatui and demonstrates a technique for updating widget state during the rendering cycle to create animations and track performance.

documents.title: Colors
documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to use and display the full range of colors supported by the Ratatui library, including named, indexed, and grayscale variants.

documents.title: Modifiers
documents.summary: This document provides a practical example of how to use text modifiers and color combinations in the Ratatui library to style terminal user interfaces.

documents.title: Style Examples
documents.summary: This document provides a directory of examples for applying colors, RGB values, and text modifiers within the Ratatui terminal user interface library.

documents.title: Barchart
documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to implement and customize vertical and horizontal bar charts using the Ratatui BarChart widget.

documents.title: Block
documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to use the Block widget in the Ratatui library to create borders, titles, and styled areas within a terminal user interface.

documents.title: Calendar
documents.summary: This document provides a practical implementation example of the Ratatui calendar widget, demonstrating how to display monthly views and style specific dates using an event store.

documents.title: Canvas
documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to use the Ratatui Canvas widget to draw shapes, maps, and interactive graphics within a terminal UI. It covers coordinate-based rendering, marker styles, and handling mouse input for dynamic drawing.

documents.title: Chart
documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to implement and configure various terminal-based data visualizations using the Ratatui Chart widget, including animated line charts, bar charts, and scatter plots.

documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to implement and customize different types of Gauge widgets in a Ratatui terminal application, including percentage-based, ratio-based, and unicode versions.

documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to implement and manage a stateful list widget in a terminal user interface using the Ratatui library. It covers rendering list items, handling selection state, and processing keyboard input for navigation and interaction.

documents.title: Paragraph
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive code example demonstrating how to use the Ratatui Paragraph widget to display and style text. It illustrates features such as text alignment, line wrapping, scrolling, and the application of various visual styles and masked spans.

documents.title: Scrollbar
documents.summary: This document demonstrates how to implement and customize vertical and horizontal scrollbars using the Scrollbar widget and ScrollbarState in Ratatui.

documents.title: Sparkline

documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive example of implementing a stateful, interactive table widget in Ratatui, featuring row and column highlighting, custom styling, and scrollbar integration.

documents.title: Tabs
documents.summary: This document provides a code example for creating and managing a tabbed interface using the Ratatui library in Rust. It demonstrates how to handle tab state, keyboard navigation, and rendering different content for each selected tab.

documents.summary: This document explains how to integrate the color-eyre crate into a Ratatui application to provide formatted error reports while ensuring the terminal state is correctly restored during panics or errors.

documents.summary: This document outlines multiple strategies for migrating projects from the tui-rs library to Ratatui, including full replacement and aliasing methods.
